response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang background, but the role requires native-level Japanese, deep C for UNIX, Linux kernel source modification, and embedded LAN-switch security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 networking) — none of which are evidenced. C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief 7-month stint over a decade ago, and there is no Japanese language proficiency listed, which is a hard requirement.
strengths: Long, varied software engineering career with Linux experience and some low-level work (C/C++ at National Instruments, audio server thesis)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship
Experience with high-throughput, low-latency systems (DLang microservices at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a required qualification for this role
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
C programming experience is minimal and dated; no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience shown

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering and Linux background
